propositions: A CI job extracts the literal CLI fenced command block from README.md and executes those exact lines end-to-end in an isolated throwaway git repo, failing the build if any command errors or changes exit code.

The gate must run the README's printed command text itself, so wrong printed invocations (e.g. session-log missing its append verb, or a stale hard-coded id) are demonstrably caught, which an independent probe cannot detect.
The golden-path job runs against a clean non-editable wheel install (pip install .) in both test.yml and gerrit-verify.yaml so packaging and console-script entry-point regressions fail the build.
The CI golden path covers the trimmed init/create/ready/claim/close subset via check_readme_quickstart.py, not the full probe-rebar.sh.
scripts/probe-rebar.sh is retained as a complementary broader smoke test but has pre-existing rot (TTY-only auto-init and stale assertions) tracked as follow-up bug slushy-commonlaw-drongo.
The gate depends on prior fixes to the session-log command and the repaired runnable quickstart block, and must land after them to avoid extracting broken commands.
